NARRATIVE Officer(s) name and star number(s): Mokos#184/1358 Response address: 718 Noyes Response date and time: 102822 1704 Nature of Call: Deceptive Practice Recorded on Body Worn Camera: Y
In Summary: On listed reported date I took a phone report in regards to a deceptive practice.
I spoke with the victim: Walker, David. He stated he was formerly employed by a company called CDJ Technologies now known as Azul-3D. Walker stated the company provided him a work credit card through Chase Visa with number As of January 2022 Walker was no longer part of the company. He electronically locked the credit card and asked the company to close the account. 1 i
On today's date (102822) he received notification from Chase that he was in debt collection with them. Walker relayed he then contacted Chase and learned his work credit card was used in June of 2022 to purchase postal items totaling 276.00. Walker stated his credit score has declined as a result.
Being that Walker was not employed in June, Walker advised he did not make the purchase nor did he authorize the purchase. Walker reported there has been an ongoing dispute between him and the company since his departure and he wished the incident to be documented.
I provided Walker a report number.

narrative.content:
Officer(s) name and star number(s): Mokos#184/1358 Response address: 718 Noyes Response date and time: 102822 1704 Nature of Call: Deceptive Practice Recorded on Body Worn Camera: Y
In Summary: On listed reported date I took a phone report in regards to a deceptive practice.
I spoke with the victim: Walker, David. He stated he was formerly employed by a company called CDJ Technologies now known as Azul-3D. Walker stated the company provided him a work credit card through Chase Visa with number As of January 2022 Walker was no longer part of the company. He electronically locked the credit card and asked the company to close the account.
On today's date (102822) he received notification from Chase that he was in debt collection with them. Walker relayed he then contacted Chase and learned his work credit card was used in June of 2022 to purchase postal items totaling 276.00. Walker stated his credit score has declined as a result.
Being that Walker was not employed in June, Walker advised he did not make the purchase nor did he authorize the purchase. Walker reported there has been an ongoing dispute between him and the company since his departure and he wished the incident to be documented.
I provided Walker a report number.
